headline: Sabah Pygmy Elephant Corridor Blocked by 14 Plantation Leases
context: The proposed Lower Kinabatangan elephant corridor connecting Tabin and Kinabatangan wildlife reserves is blocked by 14 oil palm plantation leases with terms extending to 2060.

cards.big: Sabah approved the elephant corridor plan in 2012.
cards.sub: 13 years later, 14 plantation leases still block the route. Not one lease has been renegotiated.

cards.big: Plantation leases run to 2060 — 35 more years.
cards.sub: Compulsory acquisition would cost an estimated RM620M in compensation at current land values.

cards.big: Human-elephant conflict kills 15-20 elephants annually in Kinabatangan.
cards.sub: Without a corridor, the Tabin population of 300 elephants is genetically isolated.

cards.big: RSPO requires 'no deforestation' but does not mandate corridor provision.
cards.sub: Certification standards could require corridor easements but no proposal has been tabled.

cards.big: A corridor approved in 2012 and blocked in 2026 is not a corridor. It is a line on a map.
Conservation easements — where plantations maintain ownership but allow wildlife passage — offer a middle path. Malaysia has no legal framework for them. Creating one would cost nothing.
